title: Add bead deletion auto-commit in storage
description: Extend RepositoryStorage deletion flow so bead file removals are staged and committed immediately with the same best-effort and serialized git behavior used for write commits.
acceptance_criteria: RepositoryStorage defines a deletion-specific git commit helper or equivalent focused path for removed bead files.
delete_bead stages bead file removal and creates an immediate git commit after successful deletion cleanup.
Deletion auto-commit reuses the same git serialization strategy as write auto-commit.
Deletion-related git failures remain silent and do not block bead removal.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `_git_commit_bead_deletion(bead, path)` method that stages and commits bead file deletions using the same `_git_lock` serialization and `try/except Exception: pass` silent-failure pattern as `_git_commit_bead`. Updated `delete_bead` to call it immediately after `path.unlink()`.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. Git failures remain non-fatal; the deletion itself is not affected by git commit failures.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify delete_bead triggers a git commit for the removed file, and that git failures during deletion do not raise exceptions or block the deletion.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Only `storage.py` was touched, within declared file scope.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: `git add <path>` is used on the already-deleted file path to stage the deletion — this is the correct git behavior (git tracks deletions via staging removed paths). The commit is placed before `_cleanup_deleted_dependency_references` so the primary deletion is committed atomically; follow-up writes to dependents each produce their own auto-commits via the existing `_git_commit_bead` path. A dedicated `_git_commit_bead_deletion` method was preferred over extending `_git_commit_bead` with an extra flag to keep the two paths independently readable and avoid branch complexity in the write helper.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `delete_bead` on a valid bead produces a git commit with message `[bead] {id}: deleted`; (2) git failures during deletion commit do not raise exceptions; (3) the bead file is removed from disk regardless of git outcome; (4) `_cleanup_deleted_dependency_references` still runs after a git failure. Regression: existing `delete_bead` behavior (child-check, status-check, force flag) must be unaffected.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified.

conflict_risks: None. Only `storage.py` was touched, within declared file scope.

execution_history.summary: Added `_git_commit_bead_deletion` helper to `RepositoryStorage` and wired it into `delete_bead` so bead file removals are staged and committed immediately after `path.unlink()`. Uses the same `_git_lock` serialization and silent-failure pattern as the per-write auto-commit.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
